doc.content: NVIDIA Form 10-Q for the quarterly period ended April 26, 2026 (filed 2026-05-20, accession 0001045810-26-000052). RPO: 'As of April 26, 2026, revenue related to remaining performance obligations from contracts greater than one year in length was $2.6 billion, which includes $2.3 billion from deferred revenue and $304 million, which has not yet been billed or recognized as revenue. Approximately 40% of revenue from contracts greater than one year in length will be recognized over the next twelve months.' Supply commitments (Note 10): 'Manufacturing, supply, and capacity commitments reflect data center-scale production and longer future ordering horizons across current and future product architectures... As of April 26, 2026, these commitments were $119 billion for which $95 billion will be paid in the remainder of fiscal year 2027 and the remaining balance will be paid in fiscal years 2028 through 2031.' 'Multi-year cloud service agreement commitments as of April 26, 2026, were $30 billion'; 'Other vendor commitments were $6 billion.' Inventories $25,797 million (vs $21,403M at Jan 25, 2026). Customer concentration: 'For the first quarter of fiscal year 2027, three direct customers represented 21%, 17%, and 16% of total revenue, all of which was primarily attributable to the Compute & Networking segment.' Prior year: two direct customers at 16% and 14%. 'We estimate that one AI research and deployment company contributed to a meaningful amount of our revenue by purchasing cloud services from our customers in the first quarter of fiscal year 2027.' Geography ($M): United States 63,769; Taiwan 12,006; China (including Hong Kong) 4,550 (vs 9,659 a year ago); Other 1,290; total 81,615 (vs 44,062). 'No shipments of Data Center Hopper products to China occurred during the quarter, compared with $4.6 billion in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026.' 'Gross margin increased to 74.9% for the first quarter of fiscal year 2027 compared to 60.5% for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, primarily due to the prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations.' Risk factors (updated): 'Some of our customers are developing their own ASICs and other products, including designs optimized for certain workloads that may not require all of the features and functionality our data center systems provide.' 'We have previously experienced and may continue to experience extended lead times of more than 12 months. To secure future supply and capacity, we have paid premiums, provided deposits, and entered into long-term supply agreements and capacity commitments.' Export controls: 'In April 2025, the USG informed us that it requires a license for export to China... of our H20 integrated circuits... we incurred a $4.5 billion charge in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026.' 'Beginning in February 2026, the USG granted licenses that would allow us to ship small amounts of H200 products to specific China-based customers. To date, we have not generated any revenue under the H200 licensing program... any H200 shipped under the new licensing program will be subject to a 25% tariff upon importation into the United States.' 'As of the end of the first quarter of fiscal year 2027... we were effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing/compute market, and our effective foreclosure from the China market helped our competitors build larger developer and customer ecosystems to challenge us worldwide.' USG officials also 'expressed an expectation that the USG will receive 15% or more of the revenue generated from licensed sales' (H20, beginning August 2025). Rubin: 'our Rubin platform which is expected to start shipping in the second half of fiscal year 2027.'

notes: NVIDIA FY2027 Q1 Form 10-Q (EDGAR, tier=primary). A deep extraction pulls the hard disclosures a category judge needs: customer-concentration (three direct customers at 21%/17%/16% of revenue), the $119B manufacturing/supply/capacity purchase commitments, >12-month lead times, customer-ASIC substitution risk, and China/H200 export foreclosure. Fidelity on the exact percentages and dollar figures and honest observed-vs-measured typing are what to reward; the primary-tier filing legitimately supports higher-confidence findings.
